# ToughNut Activity Report: February 27 - March 8, 2026

## Overview Summary

Over this 10-day period, your R1S "ToughNut" accumulated **1,077.9 miles** across **74 driving trips** with an average efficiency of 64%. You completed **17 charging sessions** adding **669.7 kWh** at a total cost of **$54.10**, while saving an estimated **$42.76** in gas costs.

---

## Daily Activity Breakdown

| Date | Distance | Trips | Charges | Energy (kWh) | Cost |
|------|----------|-------|---------|--------------|------|
| Feb 27 | 118.4 mi | 10 | 2 | 17.2 | $1.78 |
| Feb 28 | 70.8 mi | 4 | 3 | 122.7 | $16.89 |
| Mar 1 | 131.1 mi | 5 | 0 | — | $0 |
| Mar 2 | 160.7 mi | 8 | 3 | 195.3 | $26.35 |
| Mar 3 | 158.9 mi | 9 | 2 | 59.8 | $0 |
| Mar 4 | 206 mi | 5 | 2 | 108.2 | $0 |
| Mar 5 | 96.2 mi | 10 | 2 | 118.2 | $4.10 |
| Mar 6 | 65.7 mi | 13 | 2 | 33.8 | $3.49 |
| Mar 7 | 10 mi | 4 | 1 | 14.5 | $1.50 |
| Mar 8 | 60.2 mi | 6 | 0 | — | $0 |

---

## Narrative: The New England Road Trip

### **February 27-28: Departure Phase**
The period began with moderate local driving around your Connecticut base. On Feb 27 you logged 118 miles across 10 trips with two charging stops in Cornwall. The next day (Feb 28) saw a significant charging session in Plymouth, VT—adding 41.2 kWh over 11 hours—suggesting you were preparing for or returning from a longer journey.

### **March 1-4: The Road Trip Adventure**
This was your most active period, covering **656.7 miles** across 4 days. The charging locations tell the story:

- **March 1-2**: Heavy charging in Vermont (Ludlow, Dover) and New Hampshire with significant energy draws (83.3 kWh, 77.9 kWh overnight sessions)
- **March 2**: A major day with 160 miles driven and three charging stops—this was your most expensive charging day ($26.35)
- **March 3**: Continued north with DC fast charging in West Lebanon, NH (58.1 kWh) and Dover, VT
- **March 4**: Your **longest day at 206 miles** with DC fast charging in Montpelier, VT and Lebanon, NH, finishing with a substantial stop in Holyoke, MA (78.6 kWh in 32 minutes—likely a Tesla Supercharger at 215 kW)

This appears to be an extensive Northeast road trip, possibly reaching into northern Vermont and New Hampshire before heading south through Massachusetts.

### **March 5-6: Return and Settling In**
You returned to the Connecticut area:
- **March 5**: 96 miles driven with two home-style charging sessions in Cornwall (39.6 kWh and 31.3 kWh overnight)
- **March 6**: Only 65.7 miles across 13 short trips—indicating local errands around Cornwall with three different addresses on your route (Popple Swamp Road, Great Hollow Road, Cream Hill Road)

### **March 7-8: Quiet Weekend**
- **March 7**: Very low activity with just 10 miles across 4 short trips—typical weekend behavior
- **March 8**: Increased activity with 6 trips totaling 60 miles, including a 20.3-mile drive from Sharon to Litchfield at an excellent 96% efficiency

---

## Notable Highlights

🚗 **Longest driving day**: March 4 with 206 miles  
⚡ **Fastest charge**: March 4 in Holyoke, MA—78.6 kWh in 32 minutes at 215 kW  
🏔️ **Highest efficiency drive**: March 8 from Sharon to Litchfield at 96% efficiency (20.3 miles, regenerated 11.5 miles of range)  
📍 **Geographic reach**: Your trip spanned from Connecticut through Vermont, New Hampshire, and into Massachusetts  
💰 **Best value days**: March 3 and 4 had zero charging costs, likely using free charging infrastructure or home charging  

---

## Battery & Efficiency Notes

Your vehicle maintained good efficiency throughout the period (64% average), with the highest efficiency days being March 4 (68%) and March 5 (72%). The road trip period showed consistent charging patterns with strategic use of DC fast charging for quick top-ups during travel days, followed by slower overnight charging back home.

The data confirms a well-executed road trip with efficient energy management and minimal range anxiety.

transcript.text: look through my real data and summarize the 10 days of the rivians activity between february 27 and march 8 2026. if there were any notable trips, create a narrative of the time frame. today is mar 8 2026 and make sure that you include full 10 days, so if you don't have any drives and chargers in february its obviously false.
